brief_notes: Coherent narrative with good theoretical framing of the RIFT methodology's advantages over the boundary paradigm. Key statistics are reported in the text (p-values, t-statistics, effect sizes, correlations). However, tables are largely descriptive (participant info, stimulus parameters) rather than presenting results. No table of RIFT coherence values, no position-dependent profile table, no stimulus properties comparison table. The eye movement results table is adequate. The discussion appropriately considers alternative interpretations.
